name: Dielectric Resonator Antenna
description: A dielectric resonator antenna (DRA) consists of a high permittivity dielectric material that acts as both the resonator and radiator. The antenna operates by exciting electromagnetic modes within the dielectric resonator, which then radiate into free space. DRAs offer high radiation efficiency, wide bandwidth, and compact size with excellent temperature stability.

dielectric_constant.name: Relative Permittivity
dielectric_constant.description: Relative permittivity of the dielectric resonator material

resonator_height.name: Resonator Height
resonator_height.description: Height of the cylindrical dielectric resonator

resonator_radius.name: Resonator Radius
resonator_radius.description: Radius of the cylindrical dielectric resonator

ground_plane_size.name: Ground Plane Size
ground_plane_size.description: Size of the square ground plane

feed_position.name: Feed Position
feed_position.description: Distance of feed point from resonator center

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves selecting appropriate dielectric material, determining resonator dimensions for desired frequency, choosing excitation method, and optimizing feed coupling for impedance matching.

steps.title: Select Dielectric Material
steps.description: Choose dielectric constant based on size requirements and frequency

steps.title: Calculate Resonator Dimensions
steps.description: Determine radius and height for fundamental TE01δ mode

steps.title: Set Height-to-Radius Ratio
steps.description: Optimize aspect ratio for desired bandwidth and radiation pattern

steps.title: Design Ground Plane
steps.description: Size ground plane to minimize back radiation

steps.title: Select Feed Method
steps.description: Choose microstrip line, coaxial probe, or aperture coupling

steps.title: Optimize Feed Position
steps.description: Adjust feed location for 50-ohm impedance matching

steps.title: Fine-tune Dimensions
steps.description: Adjust resonator size for exact frequency and bandwidth

equations.inputImpedance: Rin = 377*Q*V²/(2*P) where Q is quality factor, V is modal voltage, P is power
equations.gain: G = 4*π*U/Pin where U is radiation intensity in maximum direction

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use cavity model with magnetic wall boundary conditions and coupling coefficient for feed interaction
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply equivalence principle with magnetic current sources on resonator surface using TE01δ mode field distribution

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 15
Petosa, A. 'Dielectric Resonator Antenna Handbook', Artech House
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 12
